title: hieracium bicolor subsp. sclerophyllum
intro: Hieracium bicolor subsp. sclerophyllum, commonly known as hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family, Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating skin conditions, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Hieracium bicolor subsp. sclerophyllum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
